mechanisms.name: Competitive Games
mechanisms.description: A game with two or more players and a single winner.

mechanisms.name: Cooperative Games
mechanisms.description: Players coordinate their actions to achieve a common win condition or conditions.

mechanisms.name: Team-Based Games
mechanisms.description: In team-based games, teams of players compete with one another to obtain victory.

mechanisms.name: Solo Games
mechanisms.description: A solo game is a game or game mode intended for play by a single player.

mechanisms.name: Semi-Cooperative Games
mechanisms.description: A game which ends with either no winners or the players winning as a group but a single player being recognized as the individual winner.

mechanisms.name: Single-Loser Games
mechanisms.description: A game with three or more players in which players are not assigned to static teams and which ends with only one loser.

mechanisms.name: Traitor Games
mechanisms.description: A traitor game can be seen as a kind of team game or as a cooperative game with a betrayal mechanism.

mechanisms.name: Scenario/Mission/Campaign Games
mechanisms.description: A game system that can be applied to a variety of different maps, starting resources and positions, and even different win and loss conditions.

mechanisms.name: Score-and-Reset Games
mechanisms.description: A game in which players play until reaching a stopping condition, then record scores, reset the game, and play additional rounds.

mechanisms.name: Legacy Games
mechanisms.description: A multisession game in which permanent and irreversible changes to the game state carry over from session to session.

mechanisms.name: Fixed Turn Order
mechanisms.description: Turn order is set at the start of the game and never varies.

mechanisms.name: Stat Turn Order
mechanisms.description: The turn order within each round is set by some statistic relating to the players' resources or position.

mechanisms.name: Bid Turn Order
mechanisms.description: Players bid for turn order.

mechanisms.name: Progressive Turn Order
mechanisms.description: One player has the First Player token; turn order rotates progressively.

mechanisms.name: Claim Turn Order
mechanisms.description: In each round there is a First Player and turns are taken clockwise from that player.

mechanisms.name: Pass Order
mechanisms.description: On their turn, players may either take an action or pass.

mechanisms.name: Real-Time
mechanisms.description: There are no turns; all players act simultaneously in real time.

mechanisms.name: Punctuated Real-Time
mechanisms.description: Real-time play interrupted by periodic pauses for resolution or planning.

mechanisms.name: Simultaneous Action Selection
mechanisms.description: Players plan their turn simultaneously and secretly, then reveal.

mechanisms.name: Role Order
mechanisms.description: Players secretly and simultaneously select an action, role, or priority that determines turn order.

mechanisms.name: Random Turn Order
mechanisms.description: Representatives of play pieces or players are randomized and drawn one at a time.

mechanisms.name: Action Timer
mechanisms.description: Players place owned timers on action spaces and take an action when the timer expires.

mechanisms.name: Time Track
mechanisms.description: There is a linear time track; the player furthest back always takes the next turn.

mechanisms.name: Passed Action Token
mechanisms.description: Players possess one or more Action Tokens that determine available actions.

mechanisms.name: Interleaved vs. Sequential Phases
mechanisms.description: Two different meta-turn structures describing how phases within rounds are ordered.

mechanisms.name: Lose a Turn
mechanisms.description: A meta-mechanism that causes a player to skip their turn under certain conditions.

mechanisms.name: Interrupts
mechanisms.description: A meta-mechanism allowing players to act outside normal turn order in response to events.

mechanisms.name: Action Points
mechanisms.description: A player receives a number of Action Points on their turn to spend on various actions.

mechanisms.name: Action Drafting
mechanisms.description: Players select from an assortment of actions in a shared pool.

mechanisms.name: Action Retrieval
mechanisms.description: Each player has a set of actions that must be retrieved after use before they can be used again.

mechanisms.name: Action/Event
mechanisms.description: On their turn, the player plays a card showing both Action Points and an Event.

mechanisms.name: Command Cards
mechanisms.description: Players have a hand of cards that allows them to activate and perform actions with a subset of their units.

mechanisms.name: Action Queue
mechanisms.description: Players create action queues and perform them in sequence.

mechanisms.name: Shared Action Queue
mechanisms.description: All players add actions to a central action queue.

mechanisms.name: Follow
mechanisms.description: One player selects an action; other players may follow with the same or a lesser action.

mechanisms.name: Order Counters
mechanisms.description: Players place order tokens into regions, indicating planned actions.

mechanisms.name: Rondel
mechanisms.description: The available actions are represented as pie wedges in a circle; players move a marker around it.

mechanisms.name: Action Selection Restrictions
mechanisms.description: Players are restricted in which actions they may choose based on prior choices or game state.

mechanisms.name: Variable Player Powers
mechanisms.description: Each player has special actions that only they can perform or that modify standard actions.

mechanisms.name: Once-Per-Game Abilities
mechanisms.description: Players have a special ability that they can use one time per game.

mechanisms.name: Advantage Token
mechanisms.description: One player has a token permitting a special action or action modification.

mechanisms.name: Gating and Unlocking
mechanisms.description: At certain points in the game, new actions are made available to the players.

mechanisms.name: Tech Trees/Tech Tracks
mechanisms.description: During the game, new actions become available to specific players, or existing actions are improved.

mechanisms.name: Events
mechanisms.description: Actions occur outside player control that cause immediate effects or change the game state.

mechanisms.name: Narrative Choice
mechanisms.description: Multiple action options are presented via a narrative format.

mechanisms.name: Bingo
mechanisms.description: Game elements are selected at random, and each player uses them for their own actions.

mechanisms.name: Layering
mechanisms.description: Components are placed above other components and can overlap in various ways.

mechanisms.name: Slide/Push
mechanisms.description: Players slide a token, and other tokens ahead of it are pushed along.

mechanisms.name: Matching
mechanisms.description: A player must play a card or token matching some feature on the previously played element.

mechanisms.name: Drawing
mechanisms.description: Players draw a picture and other players guess what it depicts.

mechanisms.name: High Number
mechanisms.description: Each player has a numeric strength; the higher strength wins the conflict.

mechanisms.name: Stat Check
mechanisms.description: There is a target number required to succeed at a test.

mechanisms.name: Critical Hits and Failures
mechanisms.description: Dice rolls exceeding a target number generate success, with special results for extremes.

mechanisms.name: Ratio/Combat Results Table
mechanisms.description: Attacker and Defender total their strength and consult a ratio-based results table.

mechanisms.name: Die Icons
mechanisms.description: The player rolls dice; any die showing the target icon is a success.

mechanisms.name: Card Play
mechanisms.description: Each player simultaneously or sequentially plays cards to resolve conflict.

mechanisms.name: Rock, Paper, Scissors
mechanisms.description: Three options are cyclically superior (A beats B, B beats C, C beats A).

mechanisms.name: Prisoner's Dilemma
mechanisms.description: Each player chooses between cooperating or defecting, with payoffs depending on both choices.

mechanisms.name: Alternate Removal
mechanisms.description: The player with fewer units removes one; then the other player removes one, alternating.

mechanisms.name: Physical Action
mechanisms.description: A physical action must be performed to determine the outcome.

mechanisms.name: Static Capture
mechanisms.description: Pieces are captured when another piece occupies or passes over their space.

mechanisms.name: Enclosure
mechanisms.description: Players try to surround pieces or key areas to capture them.

mechanisms.name: Minimap
mechanisms.description: When conflict is initiated, pieces move to a separate board for resolution.

mechanisms.name: Force Commitment
mechanisms.description: Players select how many forces they commit to battle in different categories.

mechanisms.name: Voting
mechanisms.description: Players vote on whether a proposed action will occur.

mechanisms.name: Player Judge
mechanisms.description: One player, the judge, decides the outcome of the action.

mechanisms.name: Targeted Clues
mechanisms.description: A player gives clues intended for some but not all players to guess.

mechanisms.name: Tiebreakers
mechanisms.description: If a resolution results in a tie, an alternate means breaks it.

mechanisms.name: Dice Selection
mechanisms.description: A player rolls multiple dice and selects one based on a rule.

mechanisms.name: Action Speed
mechanisms.description: Actions are rated for speed or initiative, determining resolution order.

mechanisms.name: Rerolling and Locking
mechanisms.description: Dice may be rerolled or locked to prevent rerolling.

mechanisms.name: Kill Steal
mechanisms.description: Players contribute toward a task, but only the player who completes it gets the benefit.

mechanisms.name: Hot Potato
mechanisms.description: Players strive to rid themselves of an item that incurs a penalty when triggered.

mechanisms.name: Flicking
mechanisms.description: Players use a finger to strike a token and propel it toward a target.

mechanisms.name: Stacking and Balancing
mechanisms.description: Players must add, remove, or rearrange objects from a stack or balancing surface.

mechanisms.name: Neighbor Scope
mechanisms.description: Actions, resources, or resolutions are shared between neighbors.

mechanisms.name: Victory Points from Game State
mechanisms.description: An event causes the game state to be evaluated relative to a scoring condition.

mechanisms.name: Victory Points from Player Actions
mechanisms.description: Players earn points by performing actions.

mechanisms.name: Temporary and Permanent Victory Points
mechanisms.description: Some Victory Points are never lost; others can change during the game.

mechanisms.name: Victory Points as a Resource
mechanisms.description: Victory Points may be spent as currency to impact the game state.

mechanisms.name: Hidden and Exposed Victory Points
mechanisms.description: Victory Points may be public or private information.

mechanisms.name: End-Game Bonuses
mechanisms.description: Players earn bonus VPs at the end of the game based on achieved conditions.

mechanisms.name: Race
mechanisms.description: The winner is the first player to reach the end of a track.

mechanisms.name: Player Elimination
mechanisms.description: The winner is the only player remaining in the game.

mechanisms.name: Fixed Number of Rounds
mechanisms.description: The game ends after a set number of rounds.

mechanisms.name: Exhausting Resources
mechanisms.description: The game end is triggered by a resource being exhausted.

mechanisms.name: Completing Targets
mechanisms.description: The game ends after a set number of targets or goals are completed.

mechanisms.name: Fixed Number of Events
mechanisms.description: The game ends after an event occurs a specified number of times.

mechanisms.name: Elapsed Real Time
mechanisms.description: The game ends after a set amount of actual time has elapsed.

mechanisms.name: Connections
mechanisms.description: The game ends when a specified number of connections are made on the board.

mechanisms.name: Circuit Breaker/Sudden Death
mechanisms.description: The game has a fixed victory condition and a special condition that can end it prematurely.

mechanisms.name: Finale
mechanisms.description: When the main game ends, a special mini-game determines the victor.

mechanisms.name: King of the Hill
mechanisms.description: Players earn points by occupying a special position on the board.

mechanisms.name: Catch the Leader
mechanisms.description: The game systems advantage trailing players or disadvantage leaders.

mechanisms.name: Tug of War
mechanisms.description: A marker moves back and forth on a track toward or away from a neutral position.

mechanisms.name: Highest Lowest
mechanisms.description: Each player's score equals the lowest value of several categories.

mechanisms.name: Ordering
mechanisms.description: The objective is to rearrange game elements from a disordered to an ordered state.

mechanisms.name: Betting and Bluffing
mechanisms.description: Players commit stakes to purchase a chance of winning based on hidden information or deception.

mechanisms.name: Push-Your-Luck
mechanisms.description: Players must decide between settling for existing gains and risking them for further rewards.

mechanisms.name: Memory
mechanisms.description: Hidden, trackable information whose tracking gives players an advantage.

mechanisms.name: Hidden Roles
mechanisms.description: One or more players are assigned roles not publicly revealed at the start.

mechanisms.name: Roles with Asymmetric Information
mechanisms.description: Players are secretly assigned roles with different win conditions and starting information.

mechanisms.name: Communication Limits
mechanisms.description: Games limit players from communicating openly with one another.

mechanisms.name: Unknown Information
mechanisms.description: Aspects of the game state are unknown to all players but lie within a known range.

mechanisms.name: Hidden Information
mechanisms.description: Aspects of the game state are hidden from all but one or a few players.

mechanisms.name: Probability Management
mechanisms.description: Players influence the probabilities of outcomes without directly determining them.

mechanisms.name: Variable Setup
mechanisms.description: The starting game state varies from game to game through changes to components or player setups.

mechanisms.name: Hidden Control
mechanisms.description: Players have hidden influence on locations or characters, revealed to perform actions.

mechanisms.name: Deduction
mechanisms.description: Reasoning by process of elimination to discover crucial information.

mechanisms.name: Induction
mechanisms.description: Players attempt to determine the rules governing a situation.

mechanisms.name: Questions and Answers
mechanisms.description: Players ask and answer questions constrained by rules.

mechanisms.name: Exchanging
mechanisms.description: Players exchange a set of assets for a different set with the bank.

mechanisms.name: Trading
mechanisms.description: Players may trade assets with each other.

mechanisms.name: Market
mechanisms.description: Players buy from or sell resources to markets where prices and quantities vary.

mechanisms.name: Delayed Purchase
mechanisms.description: Purchased items do not enter play right away but arrive on a future turn.

mechanisms.name: Income
mechanisms.description: Players gain resources at defined times.

mechanisms.name: Automatic Resource Growth
mechanisms.description: Resources held by a player automatically grow over time.

mechanisms.name: Loans
mechanisms.description: Players may take a loan from the bank to get more money.

mechanisms.name: Always Available Purchases
mechanisms.description: Certain resources are always available to purchase regardless of supply.

mechanisms.name: I Cut, You Choose
mechanisms.description: One player divides a set of resources into groups; another player chooses first.

mechanisms.name: Discounts
mechanisms.description: A player can obtain an asset at a reduced cost.

mechanisms.name: Upgrades
mechanisms.description: Assets may be upgraded to improved versions.

mechanisms.name: Random Production
mechanisms.description: Resources are generated from a random process and distributed to qualifying players.

mechanisms.name: Investment
mechanisms.description: Players own a share of an entity and earn returns based on its performance.

mechanisms.name: Ownership
mechanisms.description: Players own entities and collect benefits if others use them.

mechanisms.name: Contracts
mechanisms.description: Players fulfill contracts to earn rewards.

mechanisms.name: Bribery
mechanisms.description: Players offer bribes to get others to perform specific actions.

mechanisms.name: Increase Value of Unchosen Resources
mechanisms.description: Resources not selected by players increase in value over time.

mechanisms.name: Negotiation
mechanisms.description: Players make agreements about courses of action.

mechanisms.name: Alliances
mechanisms.description: Players have formal relationships that may change over the course of the game.

mechanisms.name: Resource Queue
mechanisms.description: Resources are in an ordered queue and can only be pulled from one end.

mechanisms.name: Open Auction
mechanisms.description: Players shout out bids as they will, with no turn order.

mechanisms.name: English Auction
mechanisms.description: An auctioneer asks for increasing bids; highest bidder wins.

mechanisms.name: Turn Order Until Pass Auction
mechanisms.description: Players may raise the bid or pass in turn order; last remaining bidder wins.

mechanisms.name: Sealed-Bid Auction
mechanisms.description: Players secretly make a bid; highest sealed bid wins.

mechanisms.name: Sealed Bid with Cancellation
mechanisms.description: Sealed bids where matching bids cancel each other out.

mechanisms.name: Constrained Bidding
mechanisms.description: A meta-mechanism that places constraints on what can be bid.

mechanisms.name: Once-Around Auction
mechanisms.description: Each player has one opportunity to bid, either passing or raising.

mechanisms.name: Dutch Auction
mechanisms.description: Price starts high and decreases until someone claims the item.

mechanisms.name: Second-Bid Auction
mechanisms.description: The winner pays the second-highest bid rather than their own.

mechanisms.name: Selection Order Bid
mechanisms.description: Players bid for the order in which they will draft lots.

mechanisms.name: Multiple-Lot Auction
mechanisms.description: Players simultaneously bid on multiple lots in parallel.

mechanisms.name: Closed-Economy Auction
mechanisms.description: All money spent in the auction goes to other players, not the bank.

mechanisms.name: Reverse Auction
mechanisms.description: Players bid to avoid taking a lot with negative effects.

mechanisms.name: Dexterity Auction
mechanisms.description: A physical act of dexterity is required to submit a valid bid.

mechanisms.name: Fixed-Placement Auction
mechanisms.description: Rules determine which lots players may bid on, with bids placed visually on a board.

mechanisms.name: Dutch Priority Auction
mechanisms.description: Prices for lots are determined based on the number of bids placed.

mechanisms.name: Bids as Wagers
mechanisms.description: Players bid that they can achieve an outcome in the ensuing gameplay.

mechanisms.name: Auction Compensation
mechanisms.description: Losing bidders receive a lesser award in place of the lot they bid on.

mechanisms.name: Standard Worker Placement
mechanisms.description: Players select actions by placing a worker on a building; the space is then blocked for others.

mechanisms.name: Workers of Differing Types
mechanisms.description: Workers differ in abilities or are valid for placement in different areas.

mechanisms.name: Acquiring and Losing Workers
mechanisms.description: Workers beyond the original complement may be acquired or lost during the game.

mechanisms.name: Workers-As-Dice
mechanisms.description: Workers are represented by dice whose pip values impact placement and actions.

mechanisms.name: Adding and Blocking Buildings
mechanisms.description: Buildings and their corresponding actions may be added or blocked during play.

mechanisms.name: Single Workers
mechanisms.description: Players control only a single primary worker and cannot acquire more.

mechanisms.name: Building Actions and Rewards
mechanisms.description: Buildings offer varying rewards to different players based on ownership and turn order.

mechanisms.name: Turn Order and Resolution Order
mechanisms.description: The order by which worker placement actions are selected and resolved creates design variations.

mechanisms.name: Tessellation
mechanisms.description: The playing field is divided into spaces to regulate movement.

mechanisms.name: Roll and Move
mechanisms.description: A randomizer determines how far to move a piece.

mechanisms.name: Pattern Movement
mechanisms.description: Pieces move in a specific pattern relative to the board grid.

mechanisms.name: Movement Points
mechanisms.description: A piece is given points to spend on movement across the board.

mechanisms.name: Resource to Move
mechanisms.description: Players expend a resource to move.

mechanisms.name: Measurement
mechanisms.description: Pieces may be moved up to a certain distance, measured by a ruler.

mechanisms.name: Different Dice
mechanisms.description: Different dice are used to move depending on unit or game state.

mechanisms.name: Drift
mechanisms.description: Two movement cards are played to move, creating a vector-like system.

mechanisms.name: Impulse
mechanisms.description: A turn is broken up into a series of small impulses for movement.

mechanisms.name: Programmed Movement
mechanisms.description: Players simultaneously program their movement, then reveal and execute.

mechanisms.name: Relative Position
mechanisms.description: The precise location of units is not tracked; only relative position matters.

mechanisms.name: Mancala
mechanisms.description: Movement is based on the number of units at the starting location.

mechanisms.name: Chaining
mechanisms.description: Pieces are stationary but are built out in chains from existing pieces.

mechanisms.name: Bias
mechanisms.description: Pieces automatically move in a certain direction or movement is easier in one direction.

mechanisms.name: Moving Multiple Units
mechanisms.description: Actions may move one or multiple units simultaneously.

mechanisms.name: Map Addition
mechanisms.description: The map is added to as it is explored.

mechanisms.name: Map Reduction
mechanisms.description: Over the course of the game, the map shrinks.

mechanisms.name: Map Deformation
mechanisms.description: The map is modified during play through rotation or shifts.

mechanisms.name: Move Through Deck
mechanisms.description: Players move through a deck of cards representing locations.

mechanisms.name: Movement Template
mechanisms.description: A defined template determines where a piece can move.

mechanisms.name: Pieces as Map
mechanisms.description: The pieces themselves compose the map.

mechanisms.name: Multiple Maps
mechanisms.description: The game takes place on multiple maps connected at defined points.

mechanisms.name: Shortcuts
mechanisms.description: A shorter route exists that may not always be available.

mechanisms.name: Hidden Movement
mechanisms.description: Movement occurs that is not visible to all players.

mechanisms.name: Absolute Control
mechanisms.description: One player has absolute control of an area through exclusive occupation.

mechanisms.name: Area Majority/Influence
mechanisms.description: Multiple players may occupy a space; benefits are based on proportional presence.

mechanisms.name: Troop Types
mechanisms.description: Different types of troops or units compete for area control with varying strengths.

mechanisms.name: Territories and Regions
mechanisms.description: Maps exhibit hierarchical relationships among spaces; control of larger areas yields strategic benefits.

mechanisms.name: Area Parameters
mechanisms.description: Parameters assigned to regions impact why players might wish to control them.

mechanisms.name: Force Projection
mechanisms.description: The impact of unit abilities on opponent decision-making even without direct engagement.

mechanisms.name: Zone of Control
mechanisms.description: Spaces adjacent to a unit impact the ability of opposing units to move or attack.

mechanisms.name: Line of Sight
mechanisms.description: Units may only see or target certain areas based on position and obstacles.

mechanisms.name: Set Valuation
mechanisms.description: The mathematical model by which designers assign values to sets of game elements.

mechanisms.name: Tile-Laying
mechanisms.description: Set-collection mechanisms with spatial elements; tiles are placed to form patterns.

mechanisms.name: Grid Coverage
mechanisms.description: Players cover a grid or fill a space using a variety of shapes.

mechanisms.name: Network Building
mechanisms.description: Sets represent ties between nodes, often as routes between destinations.

mechanisms.name: Combo Abilities
mechanisms.description: Abilities acquired separately that synergize together for powerful effects.

mechanisms.name: Trick-Taking
mechanisms.description: Players play cards in rounds called tricks, each evaluated to determine a winner.

mechanisms.name: Ladder Climbing
mechanisms.description: Players play cards that must beat the previous play in rank or quantity.

mechanisms.name: Melding
mechanisms.description: Cards in a specific relationship form a meld that can be played or scored.

mechanisms.name: Card Draw, Limits, and Deck Exhaustion
mechanisms.description: Games limit the number of cards held and manage drawing and deck depletion.

mechanisms.name: Deck Building
mechanisms.description: Players play from individual decks, improving them over time through card acquisition.

mechanisms.name: Drafting
mechanisms.description: Cards are distributed through an ordered selection process among players.

mechanisms.name: Deck Construction
mechanisms.description: Prior to play, players select the cards that will make up their deck.

mechanisms.name: Multi-Use Cards
mechanisms.description: Multiple actions shown on a card, but only one can be used at a time.

mechanisms.name: Tags
mechanisms.description: Game objects have icons identifying them as belonging to specific categories for combos and scoring.
